question: The codon sequence on mRNA is always read in 5' to 3' direction and not in 3' to 5' direction. This is because of ______. [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: The codon sequence on mRNA is always read in $5' \rightarrow 3'$ direction and not in $3' \rightarrow 5'$ direction. This is because of ______. [MHT-CET 2024]
solution: The codon sequence on mRNA is always read in $5' \rightarrow 3'$ direction and not in $3' \rightarrow 5'$ direction. This is because of distinct polarity of genetic code.

question: Considering chronology, who among the following performed earliest experiments in the discovery of genetic material? [2022]
question_latex: Considering chronology, who among the following performed earliest experiments in the discovery of genetic material? [2022]
options: B. Avery, Mc Carty and Mac Leod
solution: Griffith performed the earliest experiments (1928) in the discovery of genetic material, demonstrating the transformation principle in bacteria.

question: Gargi constructed m-RNA in laboratory. Which of the following codons will she have as initiating and terminating the m-RNA respectively? [MHT-CET 2020]
question_latex: Gargi constructed m-RNA in laboratory. Which of the following codons will she have as initiating and terminating the m-RNA respectively? [MHT-CET 2020]
solution: AUG is the start codon and UAG is the termination (stop) codon.

question: In A, B, C, D and Z form of DNA, which one is left handed?
question_latex: In A, B, C, D and Z form of DNA, which one is left handed?
solution: Z-Form of DNA is the left-handed double helix form of DNA.

question: Given below are the two statements. Statement I: In prokaryotes, there is only one replicon, however in eukaryotes, there are several replicons in tandem. Statement II: The two separated strands in a replicon of DNA are prevented from rejoining by helicase enzyme. In the light of above statements choose the correct answer from the options given below. [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: Given below are the two statements.\\ Statement I: In prokaryotes, there is only one replicon, however in eukaryotes, there are several replicons in tandem.\\ Statement II: The two separated strands in a replicon of DNA are prevented from rejoining by helicase enzyme.\\ In the light of above statements choose the correct answer from the options given below. [MHT-CET 2023]
options: A. Statement I is false but statement II is true.
B. Statement I is true but statement II is false.
C. Both statement I and statement II are false.
D. Both statement I and statement II are true.
answer: B. Statement I is true but statement II is false.
solution: Statement I is true. The two separated strands in a replicon of DNA are prevented from rejoining by SSBP (single strand binding protein), not helicase enzyme. Hence Statement II is false.

question: Basic proteins consist of more basic amino acids and these amino acids exist as a cation at the physiological pH of 7.4. Which among the following are basic amino acids? [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Basic proteins consist of more basic amino acids and these amino acids exist as a cation at the physiological pH of 7.4. Which among the following are basic amino acids? [MHT-CET 2024]
solution: Arginine and Lysine are basic amino acids which are the main components of Histone protein molecule in nucleosome.

question: The codon sequence on coding strand of transcription unit is ATG GTG AGC TAC GCG. What will be the codon sequence on mRNA formed on template strand? [MHT-CET 2016]
question_latex: The codon sequence on coding strand of transcription unit is ATG GTG AGC TAC GCG. What will be the codon sequence on mRNA formed on template strand? [MHT-CET 2016]
options: A. ATG GTG AGC TAC GCG
B. GCG TAC AGC GTG ATG
C. TAC CAC TGC ATG CGC
D. AUG GUG AGC UAC GCG
answer: D. AUG GUG AGC UAC GCG
solution: The mRNA sequence is the same as the coding strand but with U replacing T. So ATG GTG AGC TAC GCG becomes AUG GUG AGC UAC GCG.

question: Given below are the two statements. Statement-I: Only one strand in the transcription unit functions as template and is called antisense strand. Statement-II: The coding strand of transcription unit is called sense strand. In the light of above statements choose the correct answer from the options given below. [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: Given below are the two statements.\\ Statement-I: Only one strand in the transcription unit functions as template and is called antisense strand.\\ Statement-II: The coding strand of transcription unit is called sense strand.\\ In the light of above statements choose the correct answer from the options given below. [MHT-CET 2023]
options: A. Statement I is true but statement II is false.
B. Both statement I and statement II are true.
C. Statement I is false but statement II is true.
D. Both statement I and statement II are false.
answer: B. Both statement I and statement II are true.
solution: Both statement I and statement II are true with respect to transcriptional unit. The template strand is called antisense strand and the coding strand is called sense strand.

question: During DNA replication, the four types of DNA nucleotides are activated in presence of ______ enzyme. [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: During DNA replication, the four types of DNA nucleotides are activated in presence of ______ enzyme. [MHT-CET 2024]
solution: During DNA replication, the four types of DNA nucleotides are activated by ATP in presence of an enzyme phosphorylase. This results in the formation of deoxyribonucleotide triphosphates i.e. dATP, dGTP, dCTP and dTTP. The process is known as Phosphorylation.

question: Restriction enzymes actually cut DNA at specific sites by ______. [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Restriction enzymes actually cut DNA at specific sites by ______. [MHT-CET 2024]
options: A. Cutting the Nitrogen base from the fifth carbon atom of sugar
B. Dissolution of Hydrogen bonds between Nitrogen base pairs
D. Separating the phosphate from third and fifth carbon atoms
solution: Restriction enzymes actually cut DNA at specific sites by cutting the phosphodiester backbone between sugars.

question: The number of nucleotides in tRNA is ______. [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: The number of nucleotides in tRNA is ______. [MHT-CET 2024]
solution: The number of nucleotides in tRNA is 79 - 80.

question: A phenomenon, where the third base of tRNA at its 5' end can pair with a non-complementary base of mRNA is called
question_latex: A phenomenon, where the third base of tRNA at its $5'$ end can pair with a non-complementary base of mRNA is called
solution: To explain the possible cause of degeneracy of codons, Crick (1966) proposed the wobble hypothesis. This implies that the anticodons of tRNA read more than one codon on mRNA. Wobble hypothesis states that the base at $5'$ end of the anticodon is not spatially conformed as the other two bases, allowing it to form hydrogen bonds with any of several bases located at the $3'$ end of a codon.

question: Select the INCORRECT statement. [MHT-CET 2020]
question_latex: Select the INCORRECT statement. [MHT-CET 2020]
options: A. Human genome helps in disease detection and innovative therapy.
B. Genetic linkage maps determine the relative arrangement and distance between genes.
C. Human genome comprises of 80,000 to 100,000 genes.
D. Human genome project helps in understanding of human evolution.
answer: C. Human genome comprises of 80,000 to 100,000 genes.
solution: Human genome comprises 24 distinct types with 3 billion nucleotide pairs (haploid genome). Repetitive sequences comprise 50% of human genome. The statement that human genome comprises 80,000 to 100,000 genes is incorrect.

question: Which of the following is true for tRNA? [MHT-CET 2019]
question_latex: Which of the following is true for tRNA? [MHT-CET 2019]
options: A. It binds with a specific amino acid at its 3' end
B. It has five double stranded regions.
C. It has a codon at one end which recognises the anticodon on mRNA
D. It forms about 80% of total cellular RNA.
answer: A. It binds with a specific amino acid at its 3' end
solution: tRNA binds with a specific amino acid at its $3'$ end. The $3'$ end of tRNA has the CCA sequence where the amino acid attaches.

question: Beadle and Tatum used the organism ___________ in their experiment with mutagenic agent, giving one gene-One enzyme hypothesis. [MHT-CET 2020]
question_latex: Beadle and Tatum used the organism \underline{\hspace{2cm}} in their experiment with mutagenic agent, giving one gene-One enzyme hypothesis. [MHT-CET 2020]
solution: Beadle and Tatum used Neurospora crassa for their experiment in one gene one enzyme hypothesis as it is a haploid fungus that makes genetic analysis simple since recessive traits will show up in the offspring.

question: The human genome project formally began in ....A.... and was completed in ....B.... [2022]
question_latex: The human genome project formally began in ....A.... and was completed in ....B.... [2022]
solution: The Human Genome Project formally began in 1990 and was completed in 2003.

question: In chromosomes DNA is associated with I. histone protein II. non-histone protein III. acidic protein IV. neutral protein. Choose the correct combination for the statement.
question_latex: In chromosomes DNA is associated with I. histone protein II. non-histone protein III. acidic protein IV. neutral protein. Choose the correct combination for the statement.
solution: In forming the nucleosomal model of chromosomes, both histones and non-histones are together used to make DNA contract as far as possible. They are the basic positively charged proteins that stick to negatively charged DNA.

question: Operon concept was proposed by
question_latex: Operon concept was proposed by
solution: Operon concept to explain induction and repression of enzyme synthesis was first put forward by Francois Jacob and Jacques Monod (1961) of the Pasteur Institute in Paris.

question: Regions of transcriptional units are I. exon II. intron III. promoter IV. structural gene V. terminator VI. cistron
question_latex: Regions of transcriptional units are I. exon II. intron III. promoter IV. structural gene V. terminator VI. cistron
solution: Transcriptional unit contains three main regions: (i) Promoter - binding site for RNA polymerase for initiation, located toward $5'$ end (upstream); (ii) Structural Gene - codes for enzyme or protein; (iii) Terminator - region of DNA where transcription stops, located at $3'$ end (downstream).

question: Nucleic acids are the heredity material. This statement was proposed by
question_latex: Nucleic acids are the heredity material. This statement was proposed by
solution: Hertwig (1884) proposed that nucleic acids are the carrier of heredity. Friedrich Miescher first isolated nucleic acids in 1869.

question: Amino acids like cysteine and isoleucine are coded by more than one codon. This property of genetic code is called ........ [2022]
question_latex: Amino acids like cysteine and isoleucine are coded by more than one codon. This property of genetic code is called ........ [2022]
solution: When more than one codon codes for the same amino acid, this property of genetic code is called degeneracy.

question: In lac operon, the gene 'i' codes for repressor protein, the letter 'i' indicates. [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: In lac operon, the gene 'i' codes for repressor protein, the letter 'i' indicates. [MHT-CET 2024]
solution: In lac operon, the gene 'i' codes for repressor protein. The letter 'i' indicates inhibitor.

question: The amino acid acceptor end of tRNA is its A end which has unpaired B bases. [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: The amino acid acceptor end of tRNA is its \underline{A} end which has unpaired \underline{B} bases. [MHT-CET 2024]
solution: tRNA shows amino acid acceptor end ($3'$ end) having unpaired CCA bases (i.e. amino acid binding site) to which amino acid binds.

question: RNA expresses itself through
question_latex: RNA expresses itself through
solution: RNA can directly code for the synthesis of proteins, hence can easily express the characters. DNA, however, is dependent on RNA for the synthesis of proteins. The protein synthesizing machinery has evolved around RNA.

question: Which of the following bacteria were used by Griffith for his experiment? [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: Which of the following bacteria were used by Griffith for his experiment? [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: Bacteria used by Griffith for his experiment were Streptococcus pneumoniae (pathogenic strain) with two varieties S and R, which explained transformation.

question: The bulk of DNA (other than repetitive) forms the major peaks during density gradient centrifugation. The other small peaks are referred to as
question_latex: The bulk of DNA (other than repetitive) forms the major peaks during density gradient centrifugation. The other small peaks are referred to as
solution: DNA fingerprinting involves identifying the differences in some specific regions in DNA called repetitive DNA. These repetitive DNA are separated from the bulk genomic DNA as different peaks during density gradient centrifugation. The bulk DNA forms the major peak and the other small peaks are referred to as satellite DNA.

question: Name the heavy isotope used by Meselson and Stahl for proving the semiconservative mode of DNA replication.
question_latex: Name the heavy isotope used by Meselson and Stahl for proving the semiconservative mode of DNA replication.
solution: Meselson and Stahl used $^{15}N$ (heavy isotope of nitrogen) in the form of $^{15}NH_4Cl$ to prove the semiconservative mode of DNA replication.

question: When cut by the same restriction endonuclease, the DNA fragments with sticky ends can be joined with any DNA fragment that contains complementary sticky ends into a molecule of recombinant DNA by the enzyme ___________. [MHT-CET 2020]
question_latex: When cut by the same restriction endonuclease, the DNA fragments with sticky ends can be joined with any DNA fragment that contains complementary sticky ends into a molecule of recombinant DNA by the enzyme \underline{\hspace{2cm}}. [MHT-CET 2020]
solution: DNA ligase is the enzyme which joins the sticky ends of DNA, helping in joining different DNA fragments. Endonucleases cut DNA at specific locations, exonuclease cuts DNA at the ends, and polymerase helps in forming a new polymer of DNA or RNA whose sequence is complementary to the original template.

question: Modern understanding of DNA has evolved from the discovery of nucleic acid by ______. [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Modern understanding of DNA has evolved from the discovery of nucleic acid by \underline{\hspace{2cm}}. [MHT-CET 2024]
options: D. Avery, Mc Carty and MacLeod
solution: Modern understanding of DNA has evolved from the discovery of nucleic acid by Friedrich Miescher. He isolated a unique chemical substance from cell nuclei which he called nuclein, which was acidic and high in phosphorus.

question: DNA polymerization rate of DNA polymerase is
question_latex: DNA polymerization rate of DNA polymerase is
solution: The DNA polymerization rate of DNA polymerase is 3000 bp/s. Any mistake during replication would result in mutation.

question: Before the discovery of DNA, the proteins were considered as genetic material because
question_latex: Before the discovery of DNA, the proteins were considered as genetic material because
options: A. Proteins seemed to be much more diverse
B. Most of the enzymes are proteins
C. Proteins are formed from amino acids
answer: A. Proteins seemed to be much more diverse
solution: There are 20 amino acids, which can be arranged in any sequence and up to any length resulting in the development of innumerable kinds of proteins. Proteins are universally found in all types of organisms. That is why proteins were considered as genetic material before the discovery of DNA.

question: A nucleosome consists of ........ [2022]
question_latex: A nucleosome consists of ........ [2022]
options: A. DNA molecule of 200 base pairs with non-histone proteins.
B. single H1 histone molecule with DNA wrapped around it.
D. histone octamer with DNA wrapped around it.
answer: D. histone octamer with DNA wrapped around it.
solution: A nucleosome consists of a histone octamer (8 histone proteins: two copies each of H2A, H2B, H3, H4) with DNA wrapped around it.

question: Mark the odd one. [MHT-CET 2004]
question_latex: Mark the odd one. [MHT-CET 2004]
solution: UAA, UAG and UGA are all stop/termination codons. AUG is the start codon, so it is the odd one out.

question: When glucose is the normal energy source in bacterial cell ______ gene transcribes a repressor mRNA. [MHT-CET 2018]
question_latex: When glucose is the normal energy source in bacterial cell \underline{\hspace{1cm}} gene transcribes a repressor mRNA. [MHT-CET 2018]
solution: When glucose is the normal energy source in a bacterial cell, the 'i' gene (inhibitor/regulator gene) transcribes a repressor mRNA in the lac operon.

question: How are eukaryotes different from prokaryotes? [MHT-CET 2006]
question_latex: How are eukaryotes different from prokaryotes? [MHT-CET 2006]
options: D. Parental strand not participating in replication
solution: Okazaki fragments are present in eukaryotes and are smaller (100-200 nucleotides) compared to prokaryotes (1000-2000 nucleotides). This is one of the key differences in DNA replication between eukaryotes and prokaryotes.

question: To prove what can be the genetic material Griffith used ____ for his experiments. [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: To prove what can be the genetic material Griffith used \underline{\hspace{2cm}} for his experiments. [MHT-CET 2024]
solution: In 1928, a British medical officer Frederick Griffith performed an experiment on bacterium Streptococcus pneumoniae that causes pneumonia in humans and other mammals and proved transformation.

question: DNA ⇌ mRNA → Proteins. Choose the correct option for A, B, C and D in the central dogma diagram.
question_latex: DNA $\underset{C}{\overset{B}{\rightleftharpoons}}$ mRNA $\overset{A}{\rightarrow}$ Proteins. Choose the correct option for A, B, C and D.
options: A. A-Translation, B-Transcription, C-Reverse transcription, D-Replication
B. A-Translation, B-Transcription, C-Reverse transcription, D-Translation
C. A-Translation, B-Transcription, C-Replication, D-Reverse transcription
D. A-Transcription, B-Translation, C-Replication, D-Reverse transcription
answer: A. A-Translation, B-Transcription, C-Reverse transcription, D-Replication
solution: A-Translation, B-Transcription, C-Reverse transcription, D-Replication. In some viruses, formation of DNA occurs from RNA. This takes place by a special enzyme called reverse transcriptase or RNA dependent DNA polymerase.

question: Compare the statement A and B. Statement A: A monocistronic mRNA can produce several types of polypeptide chains. Statement B: The terminator codon is present on the mRNA. Select the correct description.
question_latex: Compare the statement A and B.\\ Statement A: A monocistronic mRNA can produce several types of polypeptide chains.\\ Statement B: The terminator codon is present on the mRNA.\\ Select the correct description.
options: A. Both the statements A and B are wrong
B. Statement A is correct and B is wrong
C. Statement A is wrong and B is correct
D. Both the statements A and B are correct
answer: C. Statement A is wrong and B is correct
solution: Eukaryotic mRNA is called monocistronic, formed from one gene. These contain only one initiation site, giving one mRNA–one polypeptide chain. So Statement A is wrong. The termination codon is present on mRNA molecule which stops formation of a polypeptide chain. The three termination codons are UGA, UAA and UAG. So Statement B is correct.

question: Exact number of nucleotides contained in human genome, revealed by human genome project are
question_latex: Exact number of nucleotides contained in human genome, revealed by human genome project are
solution: The exact number of nucleotides contained in the human genome revealed by the human genome project is approximately 3160.7 million. AUG (starting codon) codes for formyl methionine for prokaryotes.

question: In the processing of hnRNA, tailing involves ______. [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: In the processing of hnRNA, tailing involves \underline{\hspace{2cm}}. [MHT-CET 2024]
options: B. addition of methylated GTP at 5' end.
D. addition of methylated GTP at 3' end.
solution: In the processing of hnRNA, tailing involves polyadenylation at $3'$ end.

question: Which of the following is the first step in DNA replication? [MHT-CET 2009]
question_latex: Which of the following is the first step in DNA replication? [MHT-CET 2009]
solution: The first step in DNA replication is the formation of the template strand, where the double helix unwinds and the two strands separate to serve as templates.

question: Two different amino acids are never encoded by the same codon, this character of genetic code is called ______. [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Two different amino acids are never encoded by the same codon, this character of genetic code is called \underline{\hspace{2cm}}. [MHT-CET 2024]
solution: Two different amino acids are never encoded by the same codon. This character of genetic code is called non-ambiguous.

question: In a DNA molecule, if cytosine is 18%, then the percentage of adenine would be
question_latex: In a DNA molecule, if cytosine is 18\%, then the percentage of adenine would be
solution: By Chargaff's rule, C = G and A = T.\\ If cytosine (C) = 18%, then guanine (G) = 18%.\\ Total C + G = 36%.\\ Therefore A + T = 64%.\\ Adenine (A) = 64/2 = 32%.

question: What will be the length of a dsDNA strand, if it contains 100 base pairs? [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: What will be the length of a dsDNA strand, if it contains 100 base pairs? [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: The distance between consecutive base pairs is $3.4$ Å. The length of a dsDNA strand with 100 base pairs = $100 \times 3.4 = 340$ Å.

question: Human genome project was coordinated by
question_latex: Human genome project was coordinated by
solution: Human Genome Project was coordinated by US Department of Energy and National Institute of Health. Welcome Trust (UK) joined the project as a major partner. Later Japan, France, Germany, China and some other countries also joined it.

question: At 5' end of a polynucleotide chain, which enzyme is present?
question_latex: At $5'$ end of a polynucleotide chain, which enzyme is present?
solution: Temin described the presence of enzyme reverse transcriptase or RNA dependent DNA polymerase, which controls the synthesis of DNA from a single strand of RNA. D. Baltimore also described the presence of this enzyme in RNA tumour viruses.

question: DNA and RNA molecules are similar in their ___________. [MHT-CET 2020]
question_latex: DNA and RNA molecules are similar in their \underline{\hspace{2cm}}. [MHT-CET 2020]
solution: DNA and RNA are similar in their purines (Adenine and Guanine). Pyrimidines in DNA are Thymine and Cytosine, while pyrimidines in RNA are Uracil and Cytosine.

question: ESTs are
question_latex: ESTs are
options: A. Genes that are expressed as DNA
B. Genes that are expressed as RNA
C. Exons that are expressed as DNA
D. Introns that are expressed as RNA
answer: B. Genes that are expressed as RNA
solution: ESTs (Expressed Sequence Tags) are genes that are expressed as RNA.

question: The regions of chromosome, which are non-condensed, light stained and transcriptionally active, are called ______. [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: The regions of chromosome, which are non-condensed, light stained and transcriptionally active, are called \underline{\hspace{2cm}}. [MHT-CET 2024]
solution: The regions of chromosome which are non-condensed, light stained and transcriptionally active are called Euchromatin.

question: Semiconservative method is associated with. [MHT-CET 2009]
question_latex: Semiconservative method is associated with. [MHT-CET 2009]
solution: Semiconservative method is associated with DNA replication. Meselson and Stahl proved that DNA replication is semiconservative, where each new DNA molecule consists of one old and one new strand.

question: Assuming that the level of glucose is low, a mutation occurred in the repressor of the lac operon in E.coli, preventing the binding of the repressor to the operator. It will result in
question_latex: Assuming that the level of glucose is low, a mutation occurred in the repressor of the \textit{lac} operon in \textit{E.coli}, preventing the binding of the repressor to the operator. It will result in
options: A. Constitutive expression of the lac operon genes
B. Lack of expression or reduced expression of the lac operon genes under all circumstances
C. Expression of the genes only when lactose is present
D. Expression of the genes only when lactose is absent
answer: A. Constitutive expression of the lac operon genes
solution: If there is mutation in repressor then it is no longer suitable for binding at the operator gene. This leads to the working of lac operon continuously. This condition is called constitutive expression.

question: The packaging of chromatin at higher level requires an additional set of proteins that are collectively referred to as
question_latex: The packaging of chromatin at higher level requires an additional set of proteins that are collectively referred to as
solution: Together with histone proteins, the packaging of chromatin at higher level requires an additional set of basic proteins called non-histone proteins.

question: Considering chronology, which of the following experiments regarding genetic material was performed first? [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: Considering chronology, which of the following experiments regarding genetic material was performed first? [MHT-CET 2023]
options: B. Avery, Mc Carty and Mac Leod's
solution: Griffith's experiment (1928) is the first to explain the process of transformation in bacteria.

question: An mRNA template is I. translated to protein II. transcribed to protein III. transcribed to DNA IV. translated to DNA. Choose the correct combination.
question_latex: An mRNA template is I. translated to protein II. transcribed to protein III. transcribed to DNA IV. translated to DNA. Choose the correct combination.
solution: An mRNA template is translated to protein (by ribosomes with the help of tRNA and rRNA) and transcribed to DNA (by reverse transcriptase in retroviruses).

question: Which of the following set includes all monocyclic nitrogen bases? [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: Which of the following set includes all monocyclic nitrogen bases? [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: Purines (Adenine and Guanine) are bicyclic, while pyrimidines (Cytosine, Thymine, Uracil) are monocyclic nitrogen bases found in nucleic acids.

question: Number of nucleosomes present in human cells is?
question_latex: Number of nucleosomes present in human cells is?
solution: Approximately 200 bp are present per nucleosome.\\ Total number of base pairs in human genetic material = $6.6 \times 10^9$\\ Number of nucleosomes $= \frac{6.6 \times 10^9}{200} = 3.3 \times 10^7$ nucleosomes.

question: Central dogma of protein synthesis is
question_latex: Central dogma of protein synthesis is
solution: In 1958 Crick proposed the hypothesis of central dogma of protein synthesis. He proposed that the information present in DNA is transferred to RNA (transcription) and from RNA to protein (translation): DNA $\xrightarrow{\text{Transcription}}$ RNA $\xrightarrow{\text{Translation}}$ Protein.

question: Identify the INCORRECT statement from the following. [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Identify the INCORRECT statement from the following. [MHT-CET 2024]
options: A. The DNA strand of transcription unit having 5' → 3' polarity is called sense strand or coding strand.
B. Transcription unit is segment on DNA which is transcribed and consists of promoter, structural gene and terminator.
C. The promoter in a transcription unit is located towards 3' end of the structural gene upstream.
D. The DNA dependent RNA polymerase catalyses polymerisation only in 5' → 3' direction.
answer: C. The promoter in a transcription unit is located towards 3' end of the structural gene upstream.
solution: The promoter is located towards the $5'$ end of the structural gene, i.e., upstream. Statement C is incorrect because it says $3'$ end instead of $5'$ end.

question: Match column-I with column-II and select the correct answer from the options given below. [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: Match column-I with column-II and select the correct answer from the options given below. [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: Cistron (a) → Segment of DNA coding for a polypeptide (iii); Exon (b) → Coding sequences which appear in processed mRNA (iv); Intron (c) → Non-coding sequences which are removed by splicing (ii); Capping (d) → Addition of methylated GTP to $5'$ end of hnRNA (i).

question: Given below are two statements. Statement I: Lysine and arginine amino acids are more in number in histone proteins and both are basic in nature. Statement II: Lysine and arginine amino acids carry positive charge with them. In the light of above two statements, choose the correct option. [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: Given below are two statements.\\ Statement I: Lysine and arginine amino acids are more in number in histone proteins and both are basic in nature.\\ Statement II: Lysine and arginine amino acids carry positive charge with them.\\ In the light of above two statements, choose the correct option. [MHT-CET 2023]
options: A. Both statement I and statement II are incorrect.
B. Both statement I and statement II are correct.
C. Statement I is correct but statement II is incorrect.
D. Statement I is incorrect but statement II is correct.
answer: B. Both statement I and statement II are correct.
solution: Both statement I and statement II are correct with respect to histone proteins in nucleosome. Lysine and arginine are basic amino acids that carry positive charge and are the main components of histone proteins.

question: Male reproductive system contains a pair of A along with accessory B and C and an external D. Here A, B, C, and D refers to
question_latex: Male reproductive system contains a pair of A along with accessory B and C and an external D. Here A, B, C, and D refers to
options: C. A-urethra, B-testis, C-foreskin, D-rete testis
D. A-uterus, B-vasa deferentia, C-epididymis, D-rete testis
solution: In the absence of lactose, the promoter gene is responsible for suppressing the activity of the operator gene. The male reproductive system contains: A-testis (pair), B-glands (accessory), C-ducts (accessory), D-genitalia (external).

question: A DNA segment of length 34 nm contains 40% G and C base pairs. What will be the total number of H-bonds in this segment of DNA? [MHT-CET 2020]
question_latex: A DNA segment of length 34 nm contains 40\% G and C base pairs. What will be the total number of H-bonds in this segment of DNA? [MHT-CET 2020]
solution: 34 nm of DNA = 10 turns of coil = 100 bp.\\ G-C content = 40\%, so 40 G-C base pairs.\\ A-T content = 60\%, so 60 A-T base pairs.\\ G-C forms 3 hydrogen bonds: $40 \times 3 = 120$ H-bonds.\\ A-T forms 2 hydrogen bonds: $60 \times 2 = 120$ H-bonds.\\ Total = $120 + 120 = 240$ H-bonds.

question: Which one of the following molecule is NOT needed during translation in protein synthesis? [MHT-CET 2020]
question_latex: Which one of the following molecule is NOT needed during translation in protein synthesis? [MHT-CET 2020]
solution: All three types of RNAs (rRNA, tRNA, mRNA) play their respective important roles in protein synthesis. DNA is not needed during translation process as the genetic code is already encoded in the mRNA.

question: Time required for the formation of a peptide bond during elongation of polypeptide chain is ______. [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Time required for the formation of a peptide bond during elongation of polypeptide chain is \underline{\hspace{2cm}}. [MHT-CET 2024]
solution: Time required for the formation of a peptide bond during elongation of polypeptide chain is 0.1 second.

question: The main enzyme which uses a DNA template to catalyse the polymerization of deoxynucleotides is
question_latex: The main enzyme which uses a DNA template to catalyse the polymerization of deoxynucleotides is
solution: DNA polymerase is the main enzyme which uses a DNA template to catalyse the polymerization of deoxynucleotides. DNA polymerase III is mainly responsible for actual DNA replication in prokaryotes.

question: VNTR varies in size from
question_latex: VNTR varies in size from
solution: VNTR (Variable Number Tandem Repeats) varies in size from 0.1 to 20 kb. VNTRs refer to a class of satellite DNA, also called minisatellite DNA.

question: Given diagram of the lac operon showing an operon of inducible enzymes. Identify components and enzymes (A, B, C, D and E).
question_latex: Given diagram of the lac operon showing an operon of inducible enzymes. Identify components and enzymes (A, B, C, D and E).
options: A. A-Galactosidase, B-Permease, C-Transacetylase, D-Repressor protein, E-Inducer (lactose)
B. A-Galactosidase, B-Permease, C-Transacetylase, D-Inducer (lactose), E-Repressor protein
C. A-Galactosidase, B-Transacetylase, C-Permease, D-Repressor protein, E-Inducer (lactose)
D. A-Permease, B-Transacetylase, C-Galactosidase, D-Repressor protein, E-Inducer (lactose)
answer: A. A-Galactosidase, B-Permease, C-Transacetylase, D-Repressor protein, E-Inducer (lactose)
solution: A-β-Galactosidase, B-Permease, C-Transacetylase, D-Repressor protein, E-Inducer (lactose). When lactose acts as inducer, it binds to the repressor forming an inactive repressor, allowing RNA polymerase to transcribe lac mRNA which is polycistronic and produces all three enzymes.

question: During transcription, the nucleotide sequence of the DNA strand that is being coded is ATACG, then the nucleotide sequence in the mRNA would be
question_latex: During transcription, the nucleotide sequence of the DNA strand that is being coded is ATACG, then the nucleotide sequence in the mRNA would be
solution: The formation of mRNA from DNA is termed as transcription. The base sequence of mRNA is complementary to the template DNA strand. If the coding DNA strand has ATACG, the template strand has TATGC, and the mRNA would contain UAUGC (complementary to the template strand, with U replacing T).

question: The estimated size of genome in terms of number of base pairs in Homo Sapiens is ___. [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: The estimated size of genome in terms of number of base pairs in Homo Sapiens is \underline{\hspace{2cm}}. [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: The estimated size of genome in terms of number of base pairs in Homo Sapiens is 3.00 billion with an estimated 33,000 genes on 46 chromosomes.

question: In one nucleosome, which one of the following histone molecules is NOT double? [MHT-CET 2018]
question_latex: In one nucleosome, which one of the following histone molecules is NOT double? [MHT-CET 2018]
solution: The nucleosome core is made up of four types of histone proteins H2A, H2B, H3 and H4, each occurring in pairs (histone octamer). H1 is the linker histone and is NOT part of the octamer core, so it is not doubled in one nucleosome.

question: During translation in protein synthesis, binding of codon and anticodon occurs by ___ bond. [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: During translation in protein synthesis, binding of codon and anticodon occurs by \underline{\hspace{2cm}} bond. [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: During translation in protein synthesis, binding of codon (on mRNA) and anticodon (on tRNA) occurs by hydrogen bonds.

question: Which one of the following enzyme does NOT operate in Lac Operon? [MHT-CET 2020]
question_latex: Which one of the following enzyme does NOT operate in Lac Operon? [MHT-CET 2020]
solution: Enzyme DNAse does not operate in Lac operon. The three enzymes produced by the lac operon are β-galactosidase, permease, and transacetylase. Permease in lac operon increases the lactose permeability.

question: Transcription process in eukaryotes occurs during A and B phases of cell cycle. [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Transcription process in eukaryotes occurs during A and B phases of cell cycle. [MHT-CET 2024]
solution: $G_1$ and $G_2$ phases are mainly associated with protein synthesis, for which transcription process in eukaryotes occurs during these phases of the cell cycle.

question: Identify the INCORRECT statement with reference to transcription unit. [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: Identify the INCORRECT statement with reference to transcription unit. [MHT-CET 2023]
options: A. The terminator is located downstream at 3' end of coding strand.
B. DNA dependent RNA polymerase catalyses polymerization of nucleotides in 5' → 3' direction.
C. The promoter gene is located upstream towards the 5' end of structural gene.
D. The transcribed RNA is similar to the antisense strand except it has 'U' base in place of 'T'.
answer: D. The transcribed RNA is similar to the antisense strand except it has 'U' base in place of 'T'.
solution: The transcribed RNA is similar to the sense strand (coding strand) except it has 'U' base in place of 'T', as T is replaced by U in RNA. Statement D is incorrect because it says the RNA is similar to the antisense strand.

question: During replication, the bacteriophage attaches to ___________ of bacterial cell. [MHT-CET 2020]
question_latex: During replication, the bacteriophage attaches to \underline{\hspace{2cm}} of bacterial cell. [MHT-CET 2020]
solution: During replication of bacteriophage, it attaches to the receptors found on the cell wall of the bacterium and introduces its genome into the cytoplasm, utilizing the ribosomes of the host to synthesize its proteins.

question: What is the approximate size of nucleus in a typical mammalian cell? [MHT-CET 2017]
question_latex: What is the approximate size of nucleus in a typical mammalian cell? [MHT-CET 2017]
solution: The approximate size of the nucleus in a typical mammalian cell is $10^{-6}$ meter (approximately 1 micrometer in diameter).

question: A nucleosome along with linker DNA consists of _____ . [MHT-CET 2016]
question_latex: A nucleosome along with linker DNA consists of \underline{\hspace{2cm}}. [MHT-CET 2016]
options: A. Eight molecules of histones and 146 base pairs
B. Eight molecules of histones and 200 base pairs
C. Nine molecules of histones and 146 base pairs
D. Nine molecules of histones and 200 base pairs
answer: D. Nine molecules of histones and 200 base pairs
solution: A nucleosome along with linker DNA (called a chromatosome) consists of nine molecules of histones (8 core histones + 1 H1 linker histone) and approximately 200 base pairs of DNA.

question: Restriction endonuclease, in DNA finger printing, carries out following process. [MHT-CET 2016]
question_latex: Restriction endonuclease, in DNA finger printing, carries out following process. [MHT-CET 2016]
options: C. Loading DNA on agarose plate
solution: In DNA fingerprinting, restriction endonuclease carries out fragmentation of DNA by cutting it at specific recognition sites.

question: Southern blotting technique involves the transfer of DNA from
question_latex: Southern blotting technique involves the transfer of DNA from
solution: Southern blotting technique involves the transfer of DNA from the electrophoresis gel to a nitrocellulose or nylon membrane sheet.

question: Polysomes is a group of ___________. [MHT-CET 2020]
question_latex: Polysomes is a group of \underline{\hspace{2cm}}. [MHT-CET 2020]
solution: Polysomes are a group of ribosomes attached to mRNA. It saves time as multiple ribosomes make multiple proteins at the same time one after the other.

question: In the given diagram of chemical structure of DNA, identify the type of bonding shown by A, B and C.
question_latex: In the given diagram of chemical structure of DNA, identify the type of bonding shown by A, B and C.
options: A. A-N-glycosidic bonding, B-Phosphodiester bonding, C-Hydrogen bonding
B. A-N-glycosidic bonding, B-Phosphodiester bonding, C-Covalent bonding
C. A-N-glycosidic bonding, B-Phosphodiester bonding, C-Coordinate bonding
D. A-N-glycosidic bonding, B-Hydrogen bonding, C-Phosphodiester bonding
answer: D. A-N-glycosidic bonding, B-Hydrogen bonding, C-Phosphodiester bonding
solution: In the chemical structure of DNA: A represents N-glycosidic bonds (between nitrogenous base and sugar), B represents hydrogen bonds (between complementary base pairs), and C represents phosphodiester bonds (between successive nucleotides in the backbone).

question: "Transforming principle is DNA" was experimentally proved by [2022]
question_latex: ``Transforming principle is DNA'' was experimentally proved by [2022]
options: B. Avery, Mc Carty and Mac Leod
D. Alfred Hershey and Martha Chase
answer: B. Avery, Mc Carty and Mac Leod
solution: Avery, Mc Carty and Mac Leod (1944) experimentally proved that the transforming principle (identified by Griffith) is DNA.

question: Identify the set of correct statements: I Heterochromatin is 2 to 3 times more rich in DNA than in the euchromatin. II Euchromatin is transcriptionally most inactive. III Euchromatin regions are non-condensed regions of chromonema. IV Heterochromatic regions are localized near centromere, telomere and are intercalated. V Euchromatin regions stain light. Choose the correct option. [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Identify the set of correct statements:\\ I Heterochromatin is 2 to 3 times more rich in DNA than in the euchromatin.\\ II Euchromatin is transcriptionally most inactive.\\ III Euchromatin regions are non-condensed regions of chromonema.\\ IV Heterochromatic regions are localized near centromere, telomere and are intercalated.\\ V Euchromatin regions stain light.\\ Choose the correct option. [MHT-CET 2024]
options: A. I, III, IV and V only
C. II, III and IV only
D. III, IV and V only
answer: A. I, III, IV and V only
solution: Euchromatin regions are non-condensed (III), stain light (V), and are genetically very active (not inactive as stated in II). Heterochromatic regions are localized near centromere and telomere (IV) and heterochromatin is richer in DNA (I). Statement II is incorrect as euchromatin is transcriptionally active, not inactive.

question: In DNA fingerprinting technique, the radioactive intermediates formed during hybridization are _______ molecules. [MHT-CET 2017]
question_latex: In DNA fingerprinting technique, the radioactive intermediates formed during hybridization are \underline{\hspace{2cm}} molecules. [MHT-CET 2017]
solution: In DNA fingerprinting technique, the radioactive intermediates formed during hybridization are double-stranded DNA molecules. These are detected by autoradiography and observed as dark bands on X-ray film.

question: Genome of an organism CANNOT be altered by _______. [MHT-CET 2020]
question_latex: Genome of an organism CANNOT be altered by \underline{\hspace{2cm}}. [MHT-CET 2020]
solution: Genome cannot be altered by cloning as it is an asexual method in which gametogenesis or meiosis is not involved, so there is no chance of change in genetic material.

question: Nucleotide is made up of [MHT-CET 2010]
question_latex: Nucleotide is made up of [MHT-CET 2010]
solution: A nucleotide is made up of a nitrogenous base (purine or pyrimidine) + sugar (pentose) + phosphate group.

question: Match the process in Column-I with the action in Column-II. Choose the correct match from the options given below. [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Match the process in Column-I with the action in Column-II. Choose the correct match from the options given below. [MHT-CET 2024]
solution: Tailing (a) → Polyadenylation (II); Capping (b) → Addition of mGppp at $5'$ end of hnRNA (III); Translocation (c) → Movement of ribosome on mRNA (IV); Splicing (d) → Removal of introns (I).

question: The sequences recognized by restriction enzymes are ______ nucleotide long. [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: The sequences recognized by restriction enzymes are \underline{\hspace{2cm}} nucleotide long. [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: The sequences recognized by restriction enzymes are 4 to 8 nucleotides long and characterized by a particular type of internal symmetry (palindromic sequences).

question: According to genetic-code, some amino acids are encoded by more than one codon, this characteristic of genetic code is _____. [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: According to genetic-code, some amino acids are encoded by more than one codon, this characteristic of genetic code is \underline{\hspace{2cm}}. [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: Some amino acids are encoded by more than one codon; this characteristic of genetic code is degeneracy. One codon coding for only one amino acid is called non-ambiguous.

question: The light stained and non-condensed regions of eukaryotic chromosomes are called ___. [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: The light stained and non-condensed regions of eukaryotic chromosomes are called \underline{\hspace{2cm}}. [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: The light stained and non-condensed regions of eukaryotic chromosomes are called euchromatin. It is genetically very active and fast replicating.

question: Nucleosomes constitute the repeating unit of a structure in a nucleus called
question_latex: Nucleosomes constitute the repeating unit of a structure in a nucleus called
solution: Chromatin are thread-like stained (coloured) bodies seen in the nucleus. The nucleosomes in chromatin are seen as beads on a string when viewed under an electron microscope.

question: The length of DNA double helix molecule in a typical mammalian cell is approximately ____ meters. [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: The length of DNA double helix molecule in a typical mammalian cell is approximately \underline{\hspace{1cm}} meters. [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: The length of DNA double helix molecule in a typical mammalian cell is approximately 2.2 meters. This can be worked out by multiplying the total number of base pairs with distance between the consecutive base pairs.

question: What is the amino acid sequence encoded by the base sequence: UCA UUU UCC GGG AGU of a mRNA segment?
question_latex: What is the amino acid sequence encoded by the base sequence: UCA UUU UCC GGG AGU of a mRNA segment?
options: A. Methionine - Phenylalanine - Serine - Glycine - Serine
B. Glycine - Serine - Phenylalanine - Serine - Glycine
C. Serine - Phenylalanine - Serine - Glycine - Serine
D. Serine - Phenylalanine - Glycine - Serine - Glycine
answer: C. Serine - Phenylalanine - Serine - Glycine - Serine
solution: Decoding the mRNA codons:\\ UCA → Serine\\ UUU → Phenylalanine\\ UCC → Serine\\ GGG → Glycine\\ AGU → Serine\\ Therefore the sequence is: Serine - Phenylalanine - Serine - Glycine - Serine.

question: The two chains of DNA have antiparallel polarity named
question_latex: The two chains of DNA have antiparallel polarity named
options: D. Two chains of DNA don't have any polarity
solution: The two chains of DNA run antiparallel to each other: one strand runs $5' \rightarrow 3'$ and the other runs $3' \rightarrow 5'$. This was established by Watson and Crick model of DNA.

question: Given below are two statements. Statement-I: Autocatalytic function of DNA can be exemplified by replication of DNA. Statement-II: In eukaryotes, the unwinding of two strands of DNA is a bidirectional process but the replication of DNA is always unidirectional. In the light of above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below. [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Given below are two statements.\\ Statement-I: Autocatalytic function of DNA can be exemplified by replication of DNA.\\ Statement-II: In eukaryotes, the unwinding of two strands of DNA is a bidirectional process but the replication of DNA is always unidirectional.\\ In the light of above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below. [MHT-CET 2024]
options: A. Statement I is correct but statement II is incorrect.
B. Statement I is incorrect but statement II is correct.
C. Both statement I and statement II are correct.
D. Both statement I and statement II are incorrect.
answer: A. Statement I is correct but statement II is incorrect.
solution: Statement I is correct: Autocatalytic function of DNA can be exemplified by replication of DNA. Statement II is incorrect: In eukaryotes, the unwinding of two strands of DNA is a unidirectional process but the replication of DNA is always bidirectional.

question: Nucleoside is formed when the nitrogenous bases are linked to
question_latex: Nucleoside is formed when the nitrogenous bases are linked to
solution: Nucleoside is formed when a nitrogenous base is linked to a pentose sugar (deoxyribose or ribose) via an N-glycosidic bond. When a phosphate group is added to a nucleoside, it becomes a nucleotide.

question: The noncondensed, light stained and transcriptionally active segments of chromosomes are called ______. [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: The noncondensed, light stained and transcriptionally active segments of chromosomes are called \underline{\hspace{2cm}}. [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: The noncondensed, light stained and transcriptionally active segments of chromosomes are called euchromatin. Dark stained and transcriptionally less active segments are called heterochromatin.

question: The anticodon is located on ___. [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: The anticodon is located on \underline{\hspace{1cm}}. [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: The anticodon is located on tRNA. tRNA has bases complementary to the codon (on mRNA). This molecule is called an adaptor as it reads the codon and simultaneously binds with the amino acid.

question: Double helix model of DNA structure was given by I. James Watson II. Francis Crick III. Maurice Wilkins IV. Rosalind Franklin. Choose the correct option.
question_latex: Double helix model of DNA structure was given by I. James Watson II. Francis Crick III. Maurice Wilkins IV. Rosalind Franklin. Choose the correct option.
solution: The double helix model of DNA was proposed by James Watson and Francis Crick in 1953, based on X-ray diffraction data produced by Maurice Wilkins and Rosalind Franklin. Haploid content of human DNA has $3.3 \times 10^9$ bp.

question: During transcription, the DNA strand which acts as a template is called A strand and it has B polarity. [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: During transcription, the DNA strand which acts as a template is called \underline{A} strand and it has \underline{B} polarity. [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: In a transcription unit, the DNA strand which acts as a template is called the Antisense strand with $3' \rightarrow 5'$ polarity. The other strand is called the coding strand or sense strand with $5' \rightarrow 3'$ polarity.
